Longetia montana, also known as climbing milkwort or monkey ladder, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often utilized in herbal preparations. Traditionally, the leaves of Longetia montana are used to make teas, infusions, and other remedies for treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some parts of the world, the plant is also used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. However, the effectiveness and safety of these herbal preparations require further scientific investigation.
